textPlain: I couldn't find a list anywhere, does this currently run on any hardware?
Interesting. But who is OpenDevicePartnership?Looking at the members on the repository this seems to be a Microsoft project?
Can this be an alternative to TianoCore for qemu/kvm set ups?

People like to shit on UEFI, but I had an old motherboard which didn't support booting from NVMe drives.I found out instructions on how to download the BIOS for a newer motherboard from the same manufacturer, use some tool to extract the NVMe driver from it (it had a filesystem inside), then insert it into the BIOS update file of my motherboard, flashed that, and it worked.Maybe this would not work today, if the whole BIOS update file is signed, I don't know what the current practices are.

Can one even do UEFI firmware projects without at least keeping Microsoft in the loop?As far as I remmeber, they control the issuance of keys for bootloaders.
Or is this project supposed to do away with that?
